title: temporalCPSA Overview
text: Cross-Population Survival Analysis (CPSA) framework assessing prognostic molecular trajectories across complementary patient cohorts. CPSA estimates temporal molecular patterns (TMPs) deeply profiled discovery cohorts, evaluates clinical relevance external clinically annotated populations, validates prognostic patterns back discovery cohort. leveraging age-associated molecular structure rather survival associations observed within single cohort alone, CPSA supports cross-population prioritization prognostic signals may reflect conserved biological dynamics across heterogeneous datasets. practice, CPSA uses temporal molecular structure support prognostic biomarker prioritization biological interpretation.

what: What Can This Package Be Used For?
title: temporalCPSA Overview
text: Reproducing analyses Tignor et al., Proteogenomic analysis pediatric AYA high-grade glioma, using companion Wang Lab manuscript repository. Using deeply profiled CPTAC related multi-omic cohorts inform survival modeling external clinically annotated populations sparse absent molecular data. Modeling age-associated molecular variation comparing trajectory patterns across cohorts, example tumor reference tissues across analysis strata sex. Exploring cohort structure contiguous segmentation age-ordered molecular profiles, strategy may extend ordered biological longitudinal axes. Comparing temporal molecular programs across heterogeneous multi-omic data types, including transcriptomic, proteomic, phosphoproteomic, glycoproteomic, genomic feature spaces.

title: temporalCPSA Overview
text: basic trajectory-estimation helper ageTMP_predict_tumor_trajectory_matrix(). fits age-associated molecular trajectories deeply profiled cohort predicts trajectory values ages target cohort. target cohort can cohort, external clinically annotated cohort, age grid used visualization. tumor-versus-normal/reference trajectory comparisons, use ageTMP_compare_normal_tumor_trajectory() tumor cohort normal/reference cohort available: standard Cox model without age-class interaction terms, set age_class_col, age_reference, strata NULL:
code: library(temporalCPSA)  tumor_matrix <- your_feature_by_sample_matrix discovery_clinical <- your_discovery_clinical_table target_clinical <- your_target_clinical_table  trajectory_fit <- ageTMP_predict_tumor_trajectory_matrix(   tumor_mat = tumor_matrix,   tumor_metadata = discovery_clinical,   prediction_metadata = target_clinical,   tumor_sample_col = "sample_id",   tumor_age_col = "age",   tumor_sex_col = "sex",   prediction_sample_col = "sample_id",   prediction_age_col = "age",   prediction_sex_col = "sex",   span = 0.75,   prediction_scope = "all_samples",   return_trajectory = TRUE )  trajectory_matrix <- trajectory_fit$matrix trajectory_long <- trajectory_fit$trajectory trajectory_comparison <- ageTMP_compare_normal_tumor_trajectory(   tumor_mat = tumor_matrix,   normal_mat = normal_reference_matrix,   tumor_metadata = discovery_clinical,   normal_metadata = normal_reference_clinical,   tumor_sample_col = "sample_id",   normal_sample_col = "sample_id",   tumor_age_col = "age",   normal_age_col = "age",   tumor_sex_col = "sex",   normal_sex_col = "sex",   features = rownames(tumor_matrix)[1:25],   span = 0.75 ) library(temporalCPSA)  feature_matrix <- your_feature_matrix survival_data <- your_survival_data  spec <- ageTMP_cpsa_spec(   time_col = "days",   event_col = "os.status",   age_class_col = "age_class",   age_reference = "ADULT",   strata = c("PED", "ADO", "YA"),   base_covariates = c("age", "Grade", "Cortical", "Midline"),   adjustment_covariates = c("TP53_mut", "ATRX_mut", "H33A_mut", "ATM_mut") )  fit <- ageTMP_fit_reference_cpsa(   feature_matrix = feature_matrix,   survival_data = survival_data,   spec = spec ) standard_spec <- ageTMP_cpsa_spec(   age_class_col = NULL,   age_reference = NULL,   strata = NULL,   include_all_combined_test = FALSE,   base_covariates = c("age", "Grade"),   adjustment_covariates = character() )  standard_fit <- ageTMP_fit_reference_cpsa(   feature_matrix,   survival_data,   spec = standard_spec )

title: temporalCPSA Overview
text: Age-class estimation optional. Users may supply age classes, model age continuously, use temporalCPSA diagnostics explore whether AD-TMP structure supports age-contiguous intervals. manuscript, age classes chosen considering temporal molecular clustering conventional age distributions. temporalCPSA, implement data-driven diagnostic approach: candidate classes constrained ordered age intervals, AD-TMP matrix determines intervals best supported. review ordered segmentation landscape, draw green diagnostic heatmap overlay optimizer-selected, manuscript-style, investigator-selected cutpoints.
code: clinical <- ageTMP_load_discovery_clinical("data") feature_matrices <- ageTMP_load_feature_matrix(   "data",   modality = c("protein", "rna", "phospho") )  tmp_matrices <- lapply(feature_matrices, function(feature_matrix) {   ageTMP_predict_tumor_trajectory_matrix(     tumor_mat = feature_matrix,     tumor_metadata = clinical,     prediction_metadata = clinical,     tumor_sample_col = "id",     tumor_age_col = "cDisc_age",     tumor_sex_col = "cDisc_Gender",     prediction_sample_col = "id",     prediction_age_col = "cDisc_age",     prediction_sex_col = "cDisc_Gender",     return_trajectory = FALSE   )$matrix })  combined_tmp <- ageTMP_combine_tmp_matrices(tmp_matrices)  fit <- ageTMP_segment_age_classes(   tmp_matrix = combined_tmp,   sample_age = setNames(clinical$cDisc_age, clinical$id),   k = 5,   min_n = 10,   labels = c("PED", "ADO", "YA", "ADULT", "SEN") )  fit$cutpoints fit$segment_summary diagnostic <- ageTMP_segment_diagnostic_matrix(   tmp_matrix = combined_tmp,   sample_age = setNames(clinical$cDisc_age, clinical$id),   k_values = 2:8,   min_n = 10 )  ageTMP_plot_segment_diagnostic(   diagnostic,   selected_k = 5,   class_labels = c("PED", "ADO", "YA", "ADULT", "SEN"),   suggested_cutpoints = c(15, 26, 40, 62),   suggested_labels = c("PED", "ADO", "YA", "ADULT", "SEN") )

title: Cross-Population Survival Analysis via Temporal Molecular Profiles
text: package uses general cohort language public API: discovery cohort: smaller, deeply profiled tumor cohort used define age-dependent tumor molecular phenotypes candidate molecular programs; reference cohort: external larger cohort compatible molecular features, survival outcomes, clinical covariates, optional mutation adjustment covariates; feature matrix: numeric feature--sample matrix. Rows may represent protein abundance, RNA expression, glycopeptide abundance, phosphosite abundance, methylation features, pathway scores, molecular scores; CPSA: Cross-Population Survival Analysis, downstream survival modeling framework used evaluate whether discovery-cohort molecular programs show age- sex-stratified survival associations reference cohort. manuscript source files published supplementary tables retain labels cDisc file names column names. package documentation, cDisc read manuscript’s reference cohort label, requirement general use. Normal/reference DLPFC developmental data used trajectory analyses documented package extdata derived DEveLopmental Trajectory Atlas (DELTA), PMID: 30518843, available http://amp.pharm.mssm.edu/DELTA.

text: Several defaults preprocessing steps intentionally preserve original manuscript trajectory workflow rather imposing general modeling API. Loess fits use base stats::loess() defaults, matching original trajectory scripts. particular, package use loess.control(surface = "direct") manuscript reproduction. Normal/reference values adjusted score ~ pH + PMI + Ethnicity covariates supplied. Missing normal pH imputed sex-stratified fitting; ethnicity label H combined C; C used reference level. Tumor matrices centered/scaled using manuscript centering range, e.g. center_age_range = c(0, 50) Figure 2C protein trajectories. published Figure 2C protein workflow fit tumor curves using samples age <= 80 (age.cut2 = 80 proteo_tadj50.R). filtering performed reproduction script calling function. Figure 2C reproduction, sex-stratified model predicted common set tumor sample ages <= 50, ages sex modeled. Supply ages prediction_ages. Manuscript panels used feature/sex/tissue-specific adaptive loess spans. Supply data frame columns feature, sex, tissue, span, set adaptive_span = TRUE recompute supplied public source data. Confidence intervals returned fit +/- qnorm(0.975) * se ci_level = 0.95. details validated original tn_df.RData protein_tadj50_list.RData objects Figure 2C proteins CNTN1, MAPT, L1CAM; manuscript settings, generated fit, ci_lower, ci_upper values match legacy tn.df$value, tn.df$low, tn.df$hi floating-point tolerance.

title: Create a CPSA model specification — ageTMP_cpsa_spec
code: ageTMP_cpsa_spec(   time_col = "days",   event_col = "os.status",   feature_col = "feature_score",   age_class_col = "age_class_new",   age_reference = "ADULT",   strata = c("PED", "ADO", "YA"),   include_all_combined_test = TRUE,   base_covariates = c("age", "Grade", "Cortical", "Midline"),   adjustment_covariates = c("IDH1_mut", "TP53_mut", "ATRX_mut", "H33A_mut", "ATM_mut"),   fdr_method = "BY",   remove_sparse_covariates = TRUE,   sparse_covariates = NULL,   sparse_positive_level = 1,   sparse_min_count = 3,   sparse_count_method = c("numeric", "factor"),   scale_features = TRUE,   engine = c("coxph", "coxph.fit") )

title: Create a CPSA model specification — ageTMP_cpsa_spec
text: time_col Column survival_data containing survival time. event_col Column survival_data containing event status. feature_col Temporary column name used current feature. age_class_col Column containing age-class labels. Set NULL age_reference = NULL strata = NULL fit standard feature Cox model without feature--age-class interactions. age_reference Reference level age_class_col. strata Age strata test dropping feature terms. include_all_combined_test Whether add omnibus .comb.* likelihood-ratio test drops main feature feature--strata interaction terms. Legacy two-level glyco ADO scripts create column. base_covariates Clinical covariates included every model. adjustment_covariates Additional covariates, mutation calls. fdr_method Multiple-testing adjustment method passed stats::p.adjust(). remove_sparse_covariates Whether drop binary covariates positive values current model frame. sparse_covariates Optional character vector limiting covariates eligible sparse binary checks. NULL, covariates two observed values checked. sparse_positive_level Positive value used sparse binary checks. sparse_min_count Drop sparse covariates counts less equal value. sparse_count_method count sparse positive values. "numeric" coerces binary/logical covariates numeric counting. "factor" preserves legacy manuscript behavior counting table(factor(x, levels = c(0, 1)))[2], drops logical covariates Cortical Midline glyco ADO scripts. scale_features Whether row-center row-scale features inside model engine. Set FALSE supplied matrix already scaled manuscript-specific way. engine Cox model fitting engine. "coxph" uses formula-based survival::coxph() path default manuscript replication. "coxph.fit" uses model matrix survival::coxph.fit() reduce formula overhead larger feature scans.
